llm_tools_used.access_method: bohrium.com — Science Navigator academic search feature
llm_tools_used.verification_source: dp.tech/en/product/bohrium — Bohrium is a scientific research platform with AI search. The underlying model powering its AI search is DeepSeek R1 per their documentation.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Finding the main baseline methods cited in the paper and their limitations
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: What are the main baseline methods that Biggio et al. 2012 'Poisoning Attacks against SVMs' compare against? Search for Brückner & Scheffer 2009 and Kloft & Laskov 2010.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Added the random label-flip comparison and Figure 2 reference. Extended the baseline section with Kloft & Laskov 2010.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Located the exact prior work citations and their feature-space limitation through academic search.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Checked Section 1 and the references in the paper to confirm baseline citations.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Reasoning about when the proposed method would NOT outperform the baseline
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: In what scenario would the kernelised gradient approach in Biggio et al. 2012 NOT outperform the feature-space baseline methods?
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Added the computational overhead argument and the surrogate model mismatch discussion. Referenced Figure 1 top row.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Identified that linear kernels make feature-space = input-space, eliminating the core advantage of the proposed method.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Confirmed with the paper's Figure 1 top row (linear kernel results) and Section 2.2.

top_5_prompts.prompt: What are the main baseline methods that Biggio et al. 2012 compare against?
top_5_prompts.why_important: Rubric requires explicit baseline naming (2 marks).

top_5_prompts.prompt: What is the key limitation of feature-space adversarial attacks for non-linear kernels?
In what scenario would the kernelised gradient NOT outperform the feature-space baseline?
Explain why linear kernels make the feature-space and input-space equivalent
What does the paper say about random label-flip attacks as a comparison?

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for Task 1.3 of Part B.
